title: Fixing Middleware Order for a Simple Express API

statement.markdown: Fix the middleware order in the given Express API to prevent authentication errors. The API should handle GET requests to '/users' and '/users/:id', and authenticate users with a custom 'authMiddleware' function. The 'authMiddleware' function checks if the user is authenticated and returns a 401 error if not.
statement.inputFormat: The API will receive a GET request to '/users' or '/users/:id'.
statement.outputFormat: The API should return a list of users or a single user object, or a 401 error if the user is not authenticated.
statement.notes: The 'authMiddleware' function is defined below and checks if the user is authenticated.

constraints.description: Number of users in the system

examples.explanation: The API returns a list of users.
The API returns a single user object.
The API returns a 401 error because the user is not authenticated.

hints.text: Check the order of middleware in the Express API.

editorial.title: Fixing Middleware Order for a Simple Express API
editorial.intuition: The middleware order should be such that the authentication middleware is called before the route handlers.
editorial.bruteForce: Try different orders of middleware to see which one works.
editorial.betterSolution: Use the Express built-in 'use' function to specify the order of middleware.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the 'use' function to specify the order of middleware as: [authMiddleware, routes].
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The authentication middleware should always be called before the route handlers.
editorial.dryRun: Try the optimal solution and see if it works.
commonMistakes: Not specifying the order of middleware
